evidence: Repository documentation and manual Azure verification
notes: Collector health, durable evidence flow, and technician-handoff output were verified after manual Python and certificate repairs.

evidence: Protected workflow artifact and sanitized repository review
notes: Control-plane inventory found image drift, disk and NIC preservation requirements, identity turnover, and hardening gaps. Execution remains unauthorized.

evidence: GitHub Actions deployment logs and artifact demo-backend-api-30029515018-1
notes: ARM failed on Microsoft.Web/serverfarms quota. Application Insights and storage were later observed; Function plan and Function App were not established.

evidence: Artifact demo-backend-api-30029515018-2 and ARM What-If output
notes: The rerun observed backend VMs and ignored App Insights/storage leftovers. Listener health and API behavior were not verified.
Azure login and inventory succeeded; numeric-string quota parsing stopped the run before ARM validation or What-If.

evidence: GitHub Actions logs and artifact collector-demo-api-30040676542-1
notes: Readiness and ARM validation passed. What-If predicted 9 Create, 13 Modify, 11 Ignore, and 5 NoChange, including protected collector, disk, NIC, VNet, subnet, load-balancer, NSG, and public-IP changes. Deploy and verify were skipped. PR #56 subsequently introduced an isolated root; no later isolated What-If is recorded.

evidence: GitHub Actions run and artifact collector-demo-api-30044644501-1
notes: Readiness and ARM validation passed. The isolated What-If was accepted with nine creates and no protected modifications. Deployment failed because the parent CLI deployment and nested Bicep module used the same name. deployment-result.json is empty, service verification was skipped, and post-failure target-resource state is not proven. PR #59 repairs the name collision but does not authorize retry.

evidence: GitHub Actions run, artifact collector-demo-api-30050103888-1, and PR #63 review
notes: Azure OIDC, inventory, readiness, ARM validation and isolated What-If succeeded. Azure Network rejected standalone load-balancer child writes and a double VNet declaration. Verification was skipped. PR #63 repaired repository code only, and PR #65 later superseded the collector-hosted strategy.

evidence: GitHub Actions run, artifact collector-demo-api-30053018998-1, and PR #64 review
notes: The run observed existing pip-st-demo-api-mst-dev and two collector-hosted operations-NSG rules, no dedicated load balancer or VM extension, and no legacy children under the remote-access load balancer. What-If proposed 2 Create, 1 Modify, 24 Ignore and 2 NoChange; the public-IP Modify was rejected fail closed. PR #64 repaired repository reconciliation only, and PR #65 later superseded the collector-hosted strategy.
